issues.name: admin-analytics-and-billing-dashboard
issues.description:
Build an internal admin web UI for billing analytics and support operations.

## Metadata

- Category: product
- Status: future
- Passes: false

## Goals

- Primary: analytics visibility (sales, churn, failures, revenue).
- Secondary: safe admin operations (support workflows) with audit trails.

## Non-goals (initially)

- Customer-facing self-serve dashboard.
- Replacing core billing APIs.

## Notes

- Keep the surface area minimal: read-heavy analytics first, then a small set of high-confidence mutations.
- Every mutation must be gated + audited (who/what/when/why).
tasks: FOUNDATION:
[ ] Choose hosting path (serve via billing service, or separate static app + API calls)
[ ] Define admin authz model (role/entitlement) and enforce on all dashboard APIs
[ ] Add structured audit log table/events for admin actions (actor, action, target, before/after, request_id)
ANALYTICS (READ):
[ ] Define core metrics: gross sales, net sales, refunds, chargebacks, MRR, active subs, churn, failed attempts
[ ] Implement time-series queries (today, 7d, 30d) with timezone handling
[ ] Add breakdowns: processor (NMI/CCBill/Solana), product/tier, country (if available)
[ ] Add payments funnel: attempts -> succeeded -> refunded/chargeback -> net
[ ] Add dunning visibility: past_due count, retries, recovered vs churned
ADMIN OPS (MUTATIONS):
[ ] User lookup + detail view (subscriptions, payments, entitlements, processor IDs)
[ ] Support actions: cancel subscription, pause/resume, extend access, comp/refund (only where safe)
[ ] Guardrails: confirmation steps, rate limiting, and require a reason for every mutation
[ ] Safe idempotency for all admin operations (avoid double-cancel/refund)
API + DATA LAYER:
[ ] Add dedicated admin endpoints (e.g. /v1/admin/analytics/*, /v1/admin/users/*)
[ ] Add indexes/materialized views if needed for dashboard performance
[ ] Add caching strategy for expensive aggregates (optional)
UI:
[ ] Minimal dashboard pages: Overview, Payments, Subscriptions, Dunning, User Lookup
[ ] Tables: filter/sort/paginate; export CSV for key views
[ ] Detail drilldowns from charts -> filtered tables
VERIFICATION:
[ ] Unit tests for analytics queries (edge dates/timezones) and authz checks
[ ] Integration tests for a small set of admin mutations + audit logging
[ ] Manual checklist for production access controls and least privilege

issues.name: usage-based-billing (postpaid metering + invoicing)
issues.description:
Add first-class support for usage-based (metered) billing where users consume resources first and are billed after the fact.

Today, doujins-billing primarily supports prepaid-style charging (subscriptions and one-off payments) plus a credits/balance system that can be decremented at time-of-use. That covers many "usage" cases as prepaid credits.

This issue is specifically about **postpaid** usage: record usage events, aggregate them into an invoice/statement for a billing period, and collect payment after the usage occurred.

## Goals

- Support metered products (e.g. $/image, $/minute, $/GB) billed in arrears.
- Make usage accounting auditable and idempotent (no double-billing).
- Allow multiple providers of usage events (host app, worker, external systems).
- Integrate with Stripe (preferred) for invoicing when configured, but keep a processor-agnostic internal model.

## Non-goals (initially)

- Real-time streaming analytics dashboard.
- Automatic disputes/credits/chargebacks handling.

## Design sketch

- Data model:
  - `billing.usage_events` (append-only): {id, user_id, occurred_at, unit_type, units, source_type, source_id, metadata}
  - `billing.usage_meters` / `billing.usage_prices` (defines how to price units): per-unit price, currency, rounding, included units, caps.
  - `billing.usage_invoices` (period statements): {id, user_id, period_start, period_end, status, subtotal, currency, processor_invoice_id?, created_at}
  - `billing.usage_invoice_lines`: {invoice_id, meter_id, units, unit_price, amount}
  - `billing.usage_billing_runs` (progress tracking): last_processed_at, cursor, idempotency markers.

- Processing:
  - A scheduled worker (River) generates invoices for a period and transitions status: draft -> finalized -> paid/failed.
  - Idempotency: usage events must have a stable unique key (`source_type/source_id`) so replays don’t duplicate.
  - Late-arriving usage: support adjustments or carry-forward into next period (explicit strategy).

- Payment collection:
  - Stripe mode: create Invoice items or metered usage records + finalize/pay (depending on Stripe product model) and store `processor_invoice_id`.
  - Non-Stripe mode: create internal invoice record and allow manual/off-channel payment recording against it.

## Open questions

- Which unit types do we need (images, seconds, tokens, bytes)?
- Do we want hard spend caps / kill-switch when usage exceeds a threshold?
- How do we handle free tier + included units?
tasks: FOUNDATION:
[ ] Decide which usage model we support first: (A) prepaid credits only, (B) postpaid metered invoicing, or (C) both (recommended)
[ ] Define unit taxonomy + pricing rules (unit types, currency, rounding, minimums, caps)
DATA MODEL:
[ ] Add migrations for usage_events + invoice/line tables + billing_runs progress table
[ ] Ensure source-idempotency for usage events (unique (source_type, source_id))
API SURFACE (INTERNAL):
[ ] Add internal Go API for recording usage: RecordUsage(user_id, meter, units, occurred_at, source_type/source_id, metadata)
[ ] Add queries for usage totals by period for user-facing display
BILLING RUNS:
[ ] Add River worker to aggregate usage into draft invoices for a period
[ ] Add finalize step: lock invoice lines and mark ready-for-payment
[ ] Add payment collection step: Stripe invoice creation/pay when configured; otherwise leave as payable internally
INTEGRATION + TESTS:
[ ] Integration tests: idempotent usage ingest, invoice generation, late events handling, and Stripe/no-Stripe modes
[ ] Documentation: how host apps should emit usage events, and what guarantees are required
